# THRIVE E — Ethics, evidence and agents

All powers install **Available Inactive**. A dashboard launcher opens a preview only; it never performs an external action.

## PWR-21 — Ethics, Evidence, Policy & Source-Watch Navigator

**Pillar:** E<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Maintain a reviewable map of official sources, local policy, evidence, owners, conflicts and expiry without making legal or clinical determinations.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** exact use case; jurisdiction; official/public source; version/status; local policy; applicability owner; evidence; review cycle<br>
**Method:** distinguish law, regulation, guidance, platform rule and local policy; record unknowns and conflicts; map control questions and human owners; set freshness watch<br>
**Output:** Source & Policy Crosswalk, evidence gaps, applicability questions and expiry queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** legal, compliance, privacy, clinical and policy owners determine applicability and action<br>
**Prohibited:** no legal advice, compliance guarantee, regulatory submission, policy publication, evidence fabrication, attestation or concealment<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-22 — Privacy, Security, Data-Flow & Consumer-Trust Gate

**Pillar:** E<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Prepare formal review of a wellness marketing data flow while minimizing collection and prohibiting sensitive individual data in the base product.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** use case; data classes; aggregate inputs; systems/vendors; purposes; consent; retention; destinations; controls; incident and deletion routes<br>
**Method:** map data lineage and trust boundaries; verify aggregate/process-only input; minimize fields; examine access, tracking, retention, deletion, subprocessors and incident/exit needs<br>
**Output:** Privacy/Security Intake, data-flow map, control questions, blocker list and authorization queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** privacy, security, legal, compliance and system owners complete formal review<br>
**Prohibited:** no PHI, patient data, consumer-sensitive data, individual workforce data, lead/contact list, credential, production access, tracking deployment or authorization-to-operate<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-23 — Ethical AI Agent Charter, Permission & Handoff Designer

**Pillar:** E<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Define a narrow agent before any run: objective, owner, non-goals, sources, data, tools, destinations, tests, human handoffs, expiry and kill.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** one bounded objective; accountable owner; beneficiaries/affected people; data; sources; tools; destinations; time/cost budget; fallback and incident route<br>
**Method:** run ORBIT; apply least privilege; prohibit recursive delegation and permission escalation; design normal, failure, bias, injection, security and recovery tests<br>
**Output:** ORBIT Charter, PERM-P0 envelope, test plan, human-handoff map and kill/rollback/purge plan<br>
**Human checkpoint:** named independent human may approve one bounded run only after evidence passes<br>
**Prohibited:** no self-activation, self-modification, clinical/high-impact decision, publication, send, spend, booking, transaction, record write or hidden persistence<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-24 — Agent Evaluation, Incident, Kill & Retirement Controller

**Pillar:** E<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Evaluate frozen agents against usefulness, provenance, privacy, bias, safety and failure behavior while preventing self-governance.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved charter/version; test fixtures; expected outputs; run owner; proposed permission; logs; human decisions; incident/rollback routes<br>
**Method:** compare to baseline; execute synthetic tests; record pass/fail/blocked/unsupported; require human release; contain failure; verify rollback, purge and retirement<br>
**Output:** Evaluation Scorecard, run receipt, exception log, kill proof, rollback/purge proof and retirement shell<br>
**Human checkpoint:** independent named humans authorize release, kill, rollback and retirement<br>
**Prohibited:** no self-attestation as sole evidence, silent retry, hidden copy, permission increase, evidence deletion, production release or incident suppression<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.
